# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - notify
  - deploy

.get_mr_info_template:
  before_script:
    - # Extract MR Title from merge commit message
    - MR_TITLE=$(echo "$CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE" | awk 'NR==3 {print}' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
    - echo "Extracted MR Title: '$MR_TITLE'"
    - # Extract MR IID from merge commit message
    - MR_IID=$(echo "$CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE" | grep -oP 'See merge request !(\d+)' | cut -d'!' -f2)
    - echo "Extracted MR IID: '$MR_IID'"
    - # Add check if MR_TITLE and MR_IID are empty, might be a direct commit or different merge strategy
    - if [ -z "$MR_TITLE" ]; then
    -   echo "Warning: MR Title not found in commit message. Using CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA."
    -   MR_TITLE="Commit $CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA"
    - fi
    - if [ -z "$MR_IID" ]; then
    -   echo "Warning: MR IID not found in commit message."
    -   MR_IID="N/A"
    - fi
  variables:
    MR_TITLE_VAR: $MR_TITLE
    MR_IID_VAR: $MR_IID

send_mr_notification:
  stage: notify
  script:
    - echo "--- MERGE REQUEST NOTIFICATION ---"
    - echo "MR Title: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE"
    - echo "MR Description: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION"
    - echo "MR Link: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_PROJECT_URL/-/merge_requests/$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID"
    # Example for sending a notification (e.g., to Slack, Teams)
    # - /usr/bin/curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' --data '{"text":"Merge Request #'$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID' ($CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE) is ready for review. Link: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_PROJECT_URL/-/merge_requests/$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID"}' $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL
  rules:
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"

deploy_application:
  stage: deploy
  extends: .get_mr_info_template # Uses the extracted MR_TITLE_VAR and MR_IID_VAR
  script:
    - echo "--- DEPLOYMENT ---"
    - echo "Deploying application based on MR title: $MR_TITLE_VAR (MR #$MR_IID_VAR)"
    - echo "Deployment description includes: '$MR_TITLE_VAR - (MR #$MR_IID_VAR)'"
    # Simulate a commit that uses MR info
    - echo "VERSION=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M)-mr$MR_IID_VAR" > VERSION
    - git config user.name "GitLab CI"
    - git config user.email "gitlab-ci@example.com"
    - git add VERSION
    - git commit -m "Deployment from MR '$MR_TITLE_VAR' (MR #$MR_IID_VAR) [ci skip]" # Uses extracted MR title
    - echo "Simulated commit to update VERSION file."
    # Create a deployment tag that includes the MR title
    - DEPLOY_TAG_NAME="deploy-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M)-mr${MR_IID_VAR//[^[:alnum:]]/}-$(echo "$MR_TITLE_VAR" | sed -r 's/[^a-zA-Z0-9]+/-/g' | cut -c 1-30 | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
    - echo "Creating deployment tag: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME"
    - git tag -a "$DEPLOY_TAG_NAME" -m "Deployment for MR: '$MR_TITLE_VAR' (MR #$MR_IID_VAR)" # Uses extracted MR title
    - # Push the new commit and tag back to the repository
    - git push origin HEAD:$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME
    - git push origin "$DEPLOY_TAG_NAME"
    - echo "Pushed new commit and tag."
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main" && $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "push"
      # Only run if it's a push to the main branch
      # This ensures we are on the target branch after a merge
      # Requires the CI_JOB_TOKEN to have write access (Developer role or higher)
      # Or configure a specific user for git push with token
  image: alpine/git:latest # An image with git for committing and tagging
